agent2 (Round 1 summarized): There is insufficient specific dental knowledge regarding how gingival embrasure sizes vary between different parts of mouth when transitioning from front towards back; thus making a definitive statement impossible based solely upon this context alone without further specialized research into oral anatomy variations across these areas. \boxed{\text{Insufficient Information}} Agent2 reasoned that since the requirements did not pertain specifically to dentistry-related facts concerning the transition of gingival embrasure dimensions along with their location within the jawline, it was necessary to conclude insufficiency due lack pertinent supporting scientific basis for such claims among general health topics discussed here instead focusing narrowly onto periodontal structures variation which wasn't covered adequately enough through presented materials hence concluding as "insufficient info". 

In summary,
- No clear pattern exists clinically describing consistent alteration in size of interdental spaces ("gingival embrasures") progressing backwardly throughout human teeth arches;
- Hence unable pinpoint exact nature/extent described query demands conclusively owing limited scope & specificity addressed herein related field expertise required but absent explicitly stated above analysis leading us toward conclusion labeled 'Insufficient Info'.
